name: agent-skills
description: Workflow skill framework for agent coding harnesses compatible with Anthropic's skill layout

skills.name: agent-md-improver
skills.description: Audit and improve agent guidance markdown files such as AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, and local overrides

skills.name: revise-agent-md
skills.description: Capture useful learnings from the current session and propose concise updates to agent guidance markdown files

skills.name: do-amend
skills.description: Amend an in-flight plan after requirements or scope change, and optionally preserve durable amendment findings in an existing wiki

skills.name: do-plan
skills.description: Create a dependency-ordered implementation plan using current repo context, relevant research, and optional wiki context

skills.name: do-start
skills.description: Execute the next unblocked task from a plan and optionally file durable findings back into an existing wiki

skills.name: do-research
skills.description: Research workspace context, APIs, and external evidence, then persist a reusable memo and optionally file durable findings into an existing wiki

skills.name: do-wiki-build
skills.description: Create or extend an Obsidian-friendly markdown wiki scaffold in the current workspace. Use this when the user wants to build a wiki, set up a knowledge base, create a research vault, or scaffold a living markdown note graph before adding sources with /do-wiki-add. Not for importing or normalizing an existing wiki-like corpus; use /do-wiki-align for that.

skills.name: do-wiki-align
skills.description: Inspect an existing wiki-like markdown corpus and align it to this repo's Obsidian-friendly note-graph conventions. Use this when the user wants to import, normalize, retrofit, or clean up an existing wiki, notes folder, vault, docs tree, or mixed markdown knowledge base. In monorepos, also use it to align relevant AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md files. Not for routine wiki maintenance; use /do-wiki-lint for that.

skills.name: do-wiki-add
skills.description: Read a local source file or intentionally save conversation context as a conversation-sourced note, then integrate it into an existing Obsidian-friendly markdown wiki. Use this when the user wants to add a source to the wiki, add a document, ingest a local note, transcript, article, report, or PDF, or explicitly preserve the current conversation as a source note. Not for proposal-first session learnings that directly update existing pages; use /do-wiki-learnings.

skills.name: do-wiki-learnings
skills.description: Review the current session for durable learnings that belong in an existing Obsidian-friendly markdown wiki. Use this when the conversation uncovered decisions, architecture facts, commands, conventions, gotchas, or open questions that future sessions should inherit, and you want a proposal-first report before editing the wiki. Not for source ingestion or correcting stale wiki claims; use /do-wiki-add or /do-wiki-amend.

skills.name: do-wiki-lint
skills.description: Run a health check on an existing Obsidian-friendly markdown wiki. Use this when the user wants to lint the wiki, health-check the knowledge base, find orphan pages, spot broken or missing cross-links, or clean up stale claims and unresolved wikilinks with safe local fixes. Not for adding new material; use /do-wiki-add or /do-wiki-learnings for that.

skills.name: do-wiki-amend
skills.description: Correct or update existing wiki content when newer evidence shows the wiki is wrong, stale, incomplete, or contradicted. Use this when the agent discovers the wiki says X but we now know Y, when code or real-world changes invalidate a wiki claim, or when the user asks to fix or amend the wiki. Not for adding new sources, routine learnings capture, structural normalization, or health checks; use /do-wiki-add, /do-wiki-learnings, /do-wiki-align, or /do-wiki-lint.

skills.name: do-wiki-review
skills.description: Surface and classify all open questions, unresolved contradictions, stale claims, and knowledge gaps from an existing wiki. Use this when the user wants to see what's still open, what needs attention, what's unresolved, or what still needs research or ingest. Not for answering questions, fixing issues, or adding new material; use /do-wiki-query, /do-wiki-lint, /do-wiki-amend, /do-wiki-add, or /do-wiki-learnings.

skills.name: do-wiki-query
skills.description: Answer questions against an existing Obsidian-friendly markdown wiki. Use this whenever the user is asking what is happening in the project, directory, codebase, architecture, workflow, decisions, or current state and the wiki likely contains the answer, even if they do not explicitly mention the wiki. Also use it for summaries, comparisons, and reusable analyses grounded in current wiki pages. Not for surfacing unresolved gaps; use /do-wiki-review for that.

skills.name: setup-obsidian-vault
skills.description: Scaffold a new Obsidian vault with pre-configured settings, keymaps, and core plugins
